MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
DEFENDANTS CATHERINE CASHIN AND THOMAS CASHIN'S MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Paul H. Alvarado, Reporter: Robert Balian, CSR # 5220 =301/PHA

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Sep-27-2010 Assigned To Trial Court

SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Oct-04-2010 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned from the Presiding Judge Dept. 206 for Court Trial/Prove-Up Hearing. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr., Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, Reporter: Lois Jones, CSR #3689.

Arlo Uriarte, Esq. appeared for Appellant/Plaintiff Martha Villagra, present with Spanish Interpreter Lorenzo Barrera and Witness Lydia Flores, all were placed under oath and direct examined.
No appearance by Respondents/Defendants Catherine and Thomas Cashin.
Exhibit No. 1 - Hand written terms & listing agreement of work conditions---------ID/EV
Exhibit No. 2 - Listing of duties and responsibilities in Spanish language--------ID/EV
Exhibit No. 3 - Spread sheet of wages with calculation and total amount due-------------ID/EV
After hearing the testimony and considering of the evidence presented, the Court taken the matter under submission for review.

Counsel to prepare a Proposed Judgment and submit to Dept. 218 for signature, and stipulated for the return of the Exhibits in open court.

Oct. 1, 2010
The Court rules in favor of Appellant/Plaintiff and against the Respondents/Defendants as follows:
Damages $46,595.81
Prejudgment interest $5,555.20, for a total judgment in the amount of $52,151.01
Attorney fees and costs are awarded pursuant to Labor Code 1194 and to be schedule on a regularly noticed motion hearing in Dept. 218.
Judgment signed by the Court.
